title: buildah in podman: statx is blocked and hence an image can't be committed
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
I am using a demo to showcase buildah inside a podman container:
```
$ sudo podman run --device=/dev/fuse --rm -v $PWD/myvol:/myvol:Z -v /var/lib/mycontainer:/var/lib/containers:Z buildah/stable buildah bud -t myimag
e /myvol
STEP 1: FROM ubi8-minimal
STEP 2: ENV foo=bar
STEP 3: LABEL colour=bright
STEP 4: COMMIT myimage
error committing container for step {Env:[foo=bar PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin container=oci  foo=bar] Command:label Args:[colour bright
] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:LABEL colour bright Original:LABEL colour=bright}: error copying layers and metadata for container "661d5701dd9fa975554b9bd12620024d2c8e61edb7
43e9f7e4fedd1c1a2c9226": Error initializing source containers-storage:ubi8-minimal-working-container: error extracting layer "53c62d3c3d425c2b9ba65f318e5c2fc6caba8698742d665
33fda92c13075a696": lstat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/87e0124d5f516dfd4f96cb5e688a30def692523669f887ff889bb602aea552cd/merged: operation not permitted
level=error msg="exit status 1"
```

I originally thought this is SELinux, but it's seccomp actually:
```
type=SECCOMP msg=audit(1582190495.537:980): auid=1000 uid=0 gid=0 ses=3 subj=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c38,c899 pid=189682 comm="fuse-overlayfs" exe="/usr/bin/fuse-ov
erlayfs" sig=0 arch=c000003e syscall=332 compat=0 ip=0x7fe228830cce code=0x50000AUID="tt" UID="root" GID="root" ARCH=x86_64 SYSCALL=statx
```

So after turning off seccomp, it builds just fine:
```
 sudo podman run --device=/dev/fuse --rm -v $PWD/myvol:/myvol:Z --security-opt seccomp=unconfined -v /var/lib/mycontainer:/var/lib/containers:Z bu
ildah/stable buildah bud -t myimage /myvol
STEP 1: FROM ubi8-minimal
STEP 2: ENV foo=bar
STEP 3: LABEL colour=bright
STEP 4: COMMIT myimage
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:b6645727229cf3a8a5b06e339e1c58c9cea1f0e3c633f2532e1a3817d0a9f886
Copying blob sha256:d60418694048324694764e8b0687aa5b546eb30bc42b657d11d8d09a37b28009
Copying blob sha256:5f70bf18a086007016e948b04aed3b82103a36bea41755b6cddfaf10ace3c6ef
Copying config sha256:63bd551f937096cff8d0b86e6c46fe43ec86f5854b65fea24e2a3bb703e57a30
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
63bd551f937096cff8d0b86e6c46fe43ec86f5854b65fea24e2a3bb703e57a30
```

Goldilocks, we need you!

```
podman-1.8.0-2.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body: Just curious, doe it work if you keep seccomp in play but use the `--privileged` option with `podman run`?
Wait, what? We block `statx`? I can't believe we do that by default...
It is definitely in the ALLOW section of our default Seccomp policy
Do you have a custom Seccomp profile installed, or have you customized the default at all?
Can you grep statx in /usr/share/containers/containers.conf  or /etc/containers/seccomp.json

Sorry for a late reply: travel.

> Do you have a custom Seccomp profile installed, or have you customized the default at all?

I don't afaik. I recall I played with seccomp some time ago, but I didn't definitely change any defaults.

> Can you grep statx in /usr/share/containers/containers.conf or /etc/containers/seccomp.json

Bot files are empty.

I can see `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json` and statx is listed as allowed there.

Edit: but the file has no effect (I moved it and was still getting the error)

Edit: no idea where should I look at this point. The only file I changed is registries.conf:
```
$ rpm -V podman containers-common                                                                                                                  
S.5....T.  c /etc/containers/registries.conf
```

```
$ grep statx /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json 
				"statx",
$ rpm -qf /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json 
containers-common-0.1.41-27.dev.git7cbb8ad.fc32.x86_64
```

Looking at the log files for containers-common.
```
rpm -q containers-common --changelog 
...
* Thu Jun 21 2018 dwalsh <dwalsh@redhat.com> - 0.1.31-6.git0144aa8
- add statx to seccomp.json to containers-config
```

interesting, so I don't understand what's wrong with my system; let's close and I will reopen if I ever hit this again

thank you for helping me out
